persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for someone who wants high-status, interesting things to attend:
- Oyster happy hours or upscale food/drink (wine dinners, tastings, chef's tables)
- Galas, fundraisers, charity benefits, receptions, award ceremonies
- Events where they can attend as a subject matter expert: public records law, open meeting law, FOIA, government transparency, civic accountability, journalism
- High-status professional networking, panels, policy forums, finance/VC/startup events
- Fashion/model events, runway shows, press events, media events, bikini/swimwear shows, fitness model showcases, promotional model events, brand activations
- Recurring upscale nightlife: scene-y bar/club nights, VIP events, boat/yacht cruises, influencer events
- Anything exclusive, influential, or scene-y

SCORE 9-10 (always attend):
- "Fashionably LATE" Thursdays at The Liberty Hotel — weekly interactive runway, models weave through crowd, free admission
- Boston Fashion Week events (Oct 4-10, 2026) and surrounding pre/post events
- SwimBoston — annual swimwear/bikini runway at Boston Harbor Distillery (21+)
- Boston En Vogue runway shows — luxury evening gowns and couture
- Mass Fashion Week showcases
- Harpoon Brewery festivals (St. Pats, Summer Games at Lawn on D, Oktoberfest)
- Boat/yacht cruises on Boston Harbor — Taylor Swift, Barbie, themed nights
- Any event at Royale Boston, ICON, VENU with 6ONE7 Productions or similar promoters
- Museum/gallery opening receptions with a cocktail hour or wine — Harvard Art Museums, MFA, ICA, Gardner — not a regular museum visit but a special evening event with a crowd
- University cultural events open to the public that draw an interesting mix of people (academics, creatives, professionals) — Harvard, MIT, BU, Northeastern

NOT wanted: pure sports games, road races, youth sports, basic trivia nights at chains.

events.name: Discussion: Reviewing Trump's (Second) First Year
events.description: Join us for an in-depth discussion analyzing the first year of Donald Trump’s second-term presidency. We’ll explore key policies, executive actions, domestic and foreign impacts, and the political dynamics that shaped this period. Participants will have the opportunity to discuss successes, controversies, and long-term implications for the country. This session encourages thoughtful, respectful dialogue across perspectives and aims to provide a nuanced understanding of the administration’s early
events.reason: Political discussion event aligned with Brian's expertise in civic/government topics. Thoughtful, respectful dialogue format suggests intellectual engagement. Good for someone interested in policy analysis and political dynamics.

events.name: The Contractor's Artificial Intelligence Playbook
events.description:
Hi Wealth Builders,

Friends of mine at the Union Club have invited us out to their invent focused on the use of Artificial Intelligence in the contracting and construction industry. This will be discussion surrounding how AI is going to impact the business and how you can stay competitive and ahead of these changes. If you can make it out, we'd love to see you there!
events.reason: AI in contracting/construction at Union Club; high-status professional venue, policy/innovation focus, real estate/business relevance for Brian

events.name: STEVE DAVIS and the WE SEE ALL STARS
events.description: Steve Davis & We See All-StarsSteve Davis tromboneEddie Henderson trumpetRalph Moore tenor saxGeorge Cables pianoEssiet Essiet bassLewis Nash drumsSets at 7:00 & 9:00pmThe idea for We See began with a simple concept: a fresh, free-flowing approach to modern jazz classics, brought to life by a dynamic sextet of world-class musicians. With a focus on both classic repertoire and creative freedom, We See reflects the essence of live jazz honest, immediate, and deeply collaborative. This sense of connection is at the heart of We See, led by Steve Davis. This performance features an all-star ensemble: Eddie Henderson (trumpet), Ralph Moore (tenor saxophone), tbds (piano), Essiet Essiet (bass), and Lewis Nash (drums).Please note: If you do not purchase all seats at a table, other customers may be seated with you.ALL TICKETS ARE NON-REFUNDABLE AND NON-EXCHANGEABLE.
events.reason: World-class jazz ensemble performance; upscale listening experience; intelligent, culturally sophisticated crowd

events.name: Ping Pong Social Hour @ Spin (Seaport)
events.description:
One of our most popular events is back, and you won’t want to miss it.

Join us for a fun night of ping pong at SPIN Boston, one of the city’s best social spots. With a full restaurant and bar, 14 ping pong tables, and no need to chase down your own balls, it’s the perfect place for a casual and energetic night out.

Come early to maximize table time and meet other group members before we start playing. Depending on turnout, table time per person may vary. Cost will also depend on final particip
events.reason: Ping Pong Social Hour at SPIN Seaport — upscale venue in desirable Seaport location, social activity with bar/restaurant, energetic crowd, proven popular event.

events.name: Join us on Luma!
events.description:
1. **We're leaving Meetup.** Meetup won't let us bring your contact info with us, so you'll need to **subscribe on Luma** to stay in the loop:
👉 [https://luma.com/bioinfoclub](https://luma.com/bioinfoclub)

Orange button. Says "**Subscribe**". 30 seconds. Do it. 💻

2. We have exciting events coming up. Next:

**AI Agents for Biological Discovery: From Spatial Omics to Therapeutics**
Sat\, April 18 \| 10:00\-11:00 AM ET
Dr. Ilknur Icke, PhD (Symbiont-AI Cognitive Labs)

See you on Luma.
The Bioin
events.reason: Bioinfo Club event on AI Agents for Biological Discovery — niche but intellectually engaging; bridges science and AI; good for interdisciplinary networking.

restaurants.name: Zuma
restaurants.editorial: High-energy Japanese izakaya in the Seaport. Known for a lively bar scene, beautiful room, and a crowd that dresses up. Popular for late dinner and drinks.

restaurants.name: Desnuda
restaurants.editorial: Intimate raw bar and natural wine spot. Industry crowd, low-key cool. East Boston location and Seaport outpost.
